summary: PR #2545 decompiled item function it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk in src/melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c by replacing a MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly block and NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback with C that calls it_8026B894 and preserves an otherwise unused comparison against the Ness yoyo item owner/reference field. The author noted the unused comparison was tricky and claimed a 100% local match; the bot report says GALE01 had no changes.

key_files.role: Only changed file; replaces inline asm/NOT_IMPLEMENTED for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with C.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c added 6 deleted 32 hunks 1
raw_diff: it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk hunk

what_changed.text: Removed the MWERKS_GEKKO asm implementation and #ifdef/#else wrapper around it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO, #pragma push, asm void it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk, #pragma pop, #else, #endif

what_changed.text: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED with C that gets the Item pointer via GET_ITEM(gobj), calls it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), then evaluates a no-op comparison between ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and ref_gobj through a volatile owner_ptr.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj); it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj); HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10; (void) ((*owner_ptr == ref_gobj) ? 0 : 0);

what_changed.text: The original asm performed a call to it_8026B894, loaded offset 0xDE4 from the Item pointer, compared it to ref_gobj, and did not branch on the result; the C preserves that unused comparison shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed asm includes bl it_8026B894, lwz r0, 0xde4(r31), cmplw r0, r30
human_pr_text: unused comparison made this one a bit tricky

smart_moves.text: The PR uses the known Item type and nessyoyo item-var union arm instead of leaving the 0xDE4 access as raw pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 replaces asm load from 0xde4(r31)

smart_moves.text: The no-op volatile pointer comparison is narrowly scoped inside a block, making the codegen steering localized to the otherwise unused compare.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local block containing HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr and the discarded ternary comparison

decomp_lessons.lesson: An unused compare visible in assembly may need to be represented explicitly in C even when its result has no semantic effect; in this PR, a volatile pointer load plus discarded ternary comparison was used to preserve the cmplw after the real helper call.
scope: matching small functions with unused comparison instructions
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: The unused comparison made this one a bit tricky, but it matches 100% on my end.
raw_diff: lwz r0, 0xde4(r31); cmplw r0, r30 replaced by volatile owner_ptr comparison

decomp_lessons.lesson: When replacing asm that loads an item field by offset, check whether an existing ItemVars union path expresses the access; here the removed 0xDE4 load maps to ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 in the submitted C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed lwz r0, 0xde4(r31)
raw_diff: added &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10

naming_conventions.name: ip
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);

naming_conventions.name: owner_ptr
naming_conventions.meaning: volatile pointer to the nessyoyo x10 HSD_GObj* field used for the preserved owner/reference comparison
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10;

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a volatile pointer to force a load for an otherwise unused field comparison.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10;
raw_diff: (void) ((*owner_ptr == ref_gobj) ? 0 : 0);
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a codegen tactic, not a general style rule; current standards require explicit build/objdiff/regression evidence for volatile or similar matching steering.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Represent an unused compare as a discarded ternary comparison rather than removing it as dead source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed cmplw r0, r30 with no branch use
raw_diff: added discarded ternary comparison
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence supports that this was used here, but does not prove it is broadly appropriate outside similar verified matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserving an unused compare with a volatile field load
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2545, it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk replaced asm containing an unused cmplw after it_8026B894 with C that takes a volatile pointer to ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and discards a comparison against ref_gobj. The author called the unused comparison tricky and claimed a 100% local match.

source_update_candidates.title: Document unused-compare codegen tactics as evidence-required
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an example under matching-tactics-need-evidence: if assembly has a compare whose result is unused, a narrowly scoped volatile load or discarded comparison may preserve codegen, but it should remain an evidence-backed matching tactic rather than general style.
